tags.name: Birth
tags.inscription: Michael Luther King Jr. was born in Atlanta, Georgia on January 15, 1929, to Mrs. Alberta King and Baptist minister Michael L. King Sr.
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:Birth Memorial at the MLK Memorial Park.jpg

tags.name: Civil Rights Movement
tags.inscription: When Rosa Parks was arrested in Montgomery for refusing to surrender her bus seat to a white passenger, community activists asked if they could meet at church to play in a bus boycott.
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:Civil Rights Movement Memorial at the MLK Memorial Park - 1.jpg

tags.name: College Education and Spiritual Growth
tags.inscription: King enrolled at Morehouse College, where he majored in sociology. Drawn to the ministry, King Jr. was ordained by his father as the Baptist minister in February 1948.
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:College Education and Spiritual Growth Memorial at the MLK Memorial Park.jpg

tags.name: Doors to Freedom
tags.inscription: Inspired by those that bravely went in search of freedom and to those who opened their doors to welcome them

tags.name: Early Life
tags.inscription: King spent his early years at 501 Auburn Avenue, about a block from Ebrnezer Baptist Church where his father was pastor.
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:Early Life Memorial at the MLK Memorial Park.jpg

tags.name: F-16 Model
tags.description: Airplane at the front of Freedom Park

tags.name: Flame of Freedom
tags.description: Brick pillar with a lamp on top. The lamp has an eagle, wings open, on its top.
tags.inscription: Flame of Freedom // American Legion Valdosta Post No. 13 // A gift to Valdosta and Lowndes County in memory of the veterans of all wars // Robert H. Haze, commander // Dedicated Veterans Day November 11, 1969

tags.name: Historical Perspective
tags.inscription: The Valdosta / Lowndes County Martin Luther King Jr. Commemorative Committee
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:Historical Perspective Sign at the MLK Memorial Park.jpg

tags.name: Hyta's Park
tags.inscription: A Valdosta city park maintained in perpetuity by the Town & Country Garden Club of Valdosta, Inc.
L.M. STANFILL // A.D. 1911
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:CPL John E. McDonald Memorial Bench in Hahira, GA.jpg
tags.name: L.M.Stanfill
Langdale Park
tags.note: All trails are closed until further notice due to Hurricane Hellene damage.

tags.name: Legacy
tags.inscription: Coretta Scott King founded the King Center in Atlanta in 1968. The center is dedicated to advancing King’s philosophies of non-violence and justice through its programs, exhibits, and tours.
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:Legacy Memorial at the MLK Memorial Park - 1.jpg

tags.name: Lowndes County Sherriff’s Office Memorial
tags.inscription: This monument is in remembrance of those who have given their lives while serving with the Lowndes County Sheriff’s Office since its inception in 1826. // May the Lord always be with them and their families.

tags.name: Maddie's Tree
tags.inscription: Maddie’s Tree // Madeline Pitts // “Don’t Count Me Out”
In Loving Memory D. Stewart DeMersseman 1979-2018
In Loving Memory David G. DeMersseman // 1955-2010

tags.description: Small urn on top of a staircase side, with a plaque with inscription in front of the urn
tags.inscription: This urn is presented to the Valdosta Junior Service League in memory of Miss Vallie Ferrell by Clarence W. & Vallie Dalton Staten and family
Beloved Brother // TSGT Derry Howell // Forever In Our Hearts

tags.name: Marriage
tags.inscription: In September 1951, King enrolled in Boston University’s PhD program in systematic theology. King met Coretta Scott while at Boston University and they were married on June 18, 1953, in Perry County, Alabama.
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:Marriage Memorial at the MLK Memorial Park.jpg

spots.name: Martin Luther King, Jr. Memorial Park

tags.name: Martin Luther King, Jr. Memorial Park
tags.description: Large obelisk with 4 sides of inscriptions quoting some of MLK Jr.’s most memorable quotes.
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:MLK Memorial Park Obelisk Inscription - 1.jpg;File:MLK Memorial Park Obelisk Inscription - 2.jpg;File:MLK Memorial Park Obelisk Inscription - 3.jpg;File:MLK Memorial Park Obelisk Inscription - 4.jpg

spots.name: In Memory of Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

tags.name: In Memory of Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.
tags.inscription: In Memory of Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. // 1929-1968 // The dream lives on…
tags.note: This is the start of the story of MLK Jr.’s life that the commemorative stones tell. Follow in a winding pattern from the “Birth” stone.
tags.wikimedia_commons: File:In-ground plaque memorial at the MLK Memorial Park.jpg

tags.name: Our Confederate Dead
tags.description: Large pedestal with multiple inscriptions and confederate symbols, atop which is a statue of a confederate soldier.
tags.inscription: 1861-1865 // Erected by Valdosta chapter, U.D.C. No. 471. // 1911. // “Lest we forget.” // Comrades // Our Confederate Dead // The principles for which they fought live eternally. // CSA
